rulings.Case Title: YING-TSUN SU VS. NATASHA V. ALEXEEVA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Order Awarding Reasonable Attorneys Fees And Costs To Ying Tsun Su Against Skybox Realty, Inc. And Paul Hwang
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 17, 2024 line 1. PLAINTIFF YING-TSUN SU Motion For Order Awarding Reasonable Attorneys Fees And Costs To Ying Tsun Su Against Skybox Realty, Inc. And Paul Hwang is DENIED. The contract's indemnification provision does not entitle Su to attorneys' fees and estoppel does not apply. =(501/JMQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: BRETT MILLIER, AS SUCCESSOR CO-TRUSTEE OF THE ET AL VS. MARC DANA MERKER, AN INDIVIDUAL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Referees Motion For Final Distribution Of Proceeds Of Sale, For Authority To Make Distribution, For Referee Fees And Costs, Request To Maintain A Closing Reserve, And Request To Be Discharged As Referee
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 17, 2024 line 2. OTHER AMY HARRINGTON (PARTITION REFEREE) Notice Of Motion And Referees Motion For Final Distribution Of Proceeds Of Sale, For Authority To Make Distribution, For Referee Fees And Costs, Request To Maintain A Closing Reserve, And Request To Be Discharged As Referee is continued to November 27, 2024 for Plaintiff to make a motion for determination and apportionment of the fees per paragraph G of February 20, 2024 order. (See May 23, 2024 Order). Plaintiff shall file and serve the motion no later than October 25, 2024 and set it for November 27, 2024 hearing. If Plaintiff does not file the motion Referee's recommendation regarding the distribution of proceeds re: attorney's fees will not be considered. =(501/JMQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TIFFANY J. SPURLOCK VS. RONALD J. HILL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For 1) Interlocutory Judgment Of Partition On The Pleadings And 2) Appointment Of Referee
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 17, 2024 line 3. PLAINTIFF TIFFANY SPURLOCK , AN INDIVIDUAL Notice Of Motion And Motion For 1) Interlocutory Judgment Of Partition On The Pleadings And 2) Appointment Of Referee is DENIED without prejudice. Moving party fails to address January 1, 2023 Partition of Real Property Act, CCP 974.311 et seq. Further, the Court is not persuaded that the order request may be obtained on a motion in a manner other than a Motion for Summary judgment/Adjudication as was the case in all analogies authorities cited by the moving party. =(501/JMQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
